Page Name: 
Baggage FAQs

                  

               






















































Additional Baggage Information

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_1.text:
If you don't see the answer to your question here, feel free to 
contact us. Expand All


Collapse All












Learn More


How Many Bags May I Carry On. You may bring one carry-on item onboard the aircraft, plus one personal item. See our 
Baggage
 page for further information. Passengers traveling on Delta Connection flights with 50 seats or less are permitted to carry personal items onboard the aircraft. Due to limited overhead and under-seat space, larger items will be tagged with a pink Gate Claim/Planeside tag and loaded in the cargo bin

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_2.text:
These bags will be returned to the passenger upon deplaning


All carry-on items must meet Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) regulations and may not exceed 22" x 14" x 9". The FAA mandates that all carry-on items fit under a seat or in an enclosed storage compartment. Our gate or flight personnel may need to further limit the amount of carry-on baggage for a particular flight, depending on the cabin storage capacity of the plane and the expected number of passengers. Are There Exceptions to the Carry-On Allowance Rule. See our 
Carry-On Baggage
 page for more information

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_3.text:
What Is the Checked Baggage Policy for SkyTeam Elite/ElitePlus Customers Traveling on Delta. To thank you for your loyalty, your first checked bag fee is waived with Delta Medallion Status or as a SkyTeamÂ® Elite & Elite Plus Member. You may qualify for additional waived bag fees and an increase in the weight of your checked bag depending on your fare type. For 
full details and baggage fees
, visit the Baggage page. Do Bag Fees Apply to All Passengers Traveling Internationally. Some bag fees apply to passengers traveling internationally

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_4.text:
The latest updates and exceptions to these fees can be found on the 
Baggage
 page. What Bag Fees Apply to International Passengers Who Have Connections/Stopovers Within the U. If all flights are on the same itinerary, international fees and rules apply to passengers traveling internationally with a connection or stopover in the U. If an international passenger travels to the U. and continues their travel within the U. on a separate ticket, domestic rules apply. Complete rules can be found on the 
Baggage
 page. Do Checked Bag Fees Apply to All U

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_5.text:
Please visit our 
checked baggage fees page
 to find the most up-to-date information by travel region. What Is the Checked Baggage Policy for Active U. Please review the baggage allowance for active-duty U. military personnel when traveling on orders or for personal travel on our 
baggage and travel fees
 page. How Can I Ensure That My Baggage Will Arrive With Me. We require outside personal identification on all checked baggage. We also recommend inside identification details, just in case the outside identification gets lost

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_6.text:
Remember to always carry medicine, money, valuable documents, keys, jewelry, cameras and other small fragile items in your carry-on luggage, just in case your baggage is separated from you for a time. And remember to never lock your luggage. If I Pack My Camera in a Suitcase, Will It Be Covered Under Delta's Liability. Unfortunately, we cannot assume liability for jewelry, cash, camera equipment or other similar valuable items contained in checked or unchecked baggage on domestic flights. See the 
Delayed, Lost or Damaged
 page for more information

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_7.text: Are Carry-On Items Included in Delta's Baggage Liability. We do not assume liability for unchecked items unless they are given to our personnel for storage during the flight or otherwise delivered into our custody for travel. Does a Walking Cane Have to Be Stowed in a Specific Area on the Aircraft. Federal regulations require that a cane must be stowed for takeoff and landings in an approved storage compartment

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_8.text:
Flexible travel canes, such as those used by customers that are visually impaired, can be stored in the area under passenger seats, as long as the cane doesn't protrude into the aisle or otherwise disturb the crew or other passengers. Expand All


Collapse All












Lost Items FAQs


I lost something on the plane or at the airport. If youâve left something on a Delta aircraft, at a Delta Sky Club, airport counter or gate, 
report your lost item
, opens in a new window
 here. Weâll do our best to locate and return it to you

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_9.text:
Visitour 
Lost and Found FAQs page
, opens in a new window
 to check your claim status or get additional help. Expand All


Collapse All












20 Minute Bag Guarantee FAQs


How Does Delta Calculate the Time the Bag Takes to Get to the Baggage Claim. The time a bag takes to arrive at the baggage claim is measured by Delta's baggage tracking system, where available. If the time elapsed between the aircraftâs arrival at the gate and delivery of the bag to the baggage claim carousel is longer than 20 minutes, the customer is eligible to earn 2,500 miles

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_10.text: This offer is valid on domestic flights within the U. 50 and Puerto Rico, marketed and operated by Delta Air Lines and Delta ConnectionÂ® carriers. What if I Checked Several Bags and Only One Is Late. If any of your bags arrive late, you are eligible for the guarantee. However, there is a limit of one request per passenger per departing or returning itinerary, regardless of number of checked bags. This offer is valid on domestic flights within the U. 50 and Puerto Rico, marketed and operated by Delta Air Lines and Delta ConnectionÂ® carriers. How Do I Request Bonus Miles if My Bag Arrives Late

delta_baggage_faqs_chunk_11.text:
com/bagsontime
 no later than two hours after your flight's arrival. Requests should only be submitted after your flight. Please allow up to two weeks after submitting your request for your bonus miles to appear in your account. This offer is valid on domestic flights within the U. 50 and Puerto Rico, marketed and operated by Delta Air Lines and Delta ConnectionÂ® carriers. Claims must be submitted within 
120 minutes
 (2 hours) after flight arrival

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_1.text:
js


































Page Name: 
Cancel or Refund Your Flight

                  

               






















































Change or Cancel






Cancel or Refund Your Flight












                  If your travel plans change, you may cancel your ticket for a refund or an eCredit depending on your ticket type and circumstances for cancellation. Cancel your trip or learn more about Deltaâs cancellation and refund policies below

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_2.text:
Cancel a Flight


































Cancellation and Refund Options












Review step-by-step guidance to help you cancel your trip and request a refund or eCredit based on your ticket type and situation below. A 
cancellation fee
 will apply in most cases, unless youâve purchased a refundable ticket type or are experiencing a 
significant schedule change
 or 
delay
 or a 
flight cancellation. Tickets not changed or canceled prior to departure will have no remaining value

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_3.text:
Expand All


Collapse All












Cancel a Non-Refundable Ticket




























If your plans change and you booked a non-refundable ticket, you can cancel your ticket for an eCredit minus any applicable 
cancellation fees. Classic and Basic tickets are nonrefundable. Please be sure to cancel your flight prior to departure. Non-refundable tickets not changed or canceled prior to departure will have no remaining value. How to Cancel a Non-Refundable Ticket 






Visitâ¯
My Trips
â¯on delta. com and select âNeed to Cancel

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_4.text:
â


Choose âStart Flight Cancellationâ


Follow the remaining steps through the cancellation process




Some experiences don't have fees for flexibility (change or cancel), such as:




Delta Main Classic and above tickets for travel within the US, Puerto Rico and the US Virgin Islands


Delta Main Classic and above tickets for travel originating in the US and Canada to anywhere in the world (including flights operated by joint venture and codeshare partners)


Basic experience tickets (Delta Main Basic and Delta Comfort Basic) are excluded




Cancellation fees will be deducted from your ticket value, and you'll receive the remainder as an eCredit

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_5.text:
Your eCredit will expire 1 year from purchase date, unless your ticket terms state otherwise. View applicable fees for your specific route on our 
Baggage & Travel Fees
 page. Cancel Non-Refundable Ticket


































Cancel an Extra or Refundable Ticket




























If you've purchased an Extra ticket or a refundable ticket and your plans change, you can cancel before your flight for a full refund. How to Cancel an Extra/Refundable Ticket




Go to 
My Trips
 on delta. com


Select your flight


Select âNeed to Cancel

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_6.text:
â


Choose âStart Flight Cancellationâ


Follow the steps to complete your cancellation




Your refund will be processed automatically to your original form of payment. If youâve already canceled your refundable ticket or requested a refund that hasnât arrived yet, you can 
check your refund status
 for updates

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_7.text:
Cancel Refundable or Extra Ticket


































Request a Refund




























If your flight was 
canceled
 or impacted by a 
significant delay
 or 
significant schedule change
â¯ (departure or arrival 3 hours or more earlier or later for domestic itineraries, or 6 hours or more for international itineraries), we automatically try to rebook you

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_8.text:
If youâre not satisfied with your rebooked flight, you can:




Search for alternative flight options in 
Fly Delta
 or â¯
My Trips
, orÂ 


Cancel
Â your rebooked flight for a refund of the unflown portion of your ticket and any pre-paid seat upgrade or Preferred Seat (and checked bag fees), or an eCredit valid for 5 years from issuance




If we canât rebook you and you take no action within 24 hours, weâll automatically refund you to your original payment method

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_9.text:
Request a Refund


Other Refund or eCredit Request Reasons


Complete the 
Travel Resolution Form
 for refund or eCredit requests such as:




Cancellation due to a Serious Communicable Disease


eCredit appeals


Fare difference for alternative flight to accommodate wheelchair or scooter


Fare difference for seat downgrade




If youâve already submitted a refund request, you can 
check your refund status

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_10.text:
Refund Restrictions


Please note, we cannot issue refunds for:




Non-refundable tickets that have not experienced a 
cancellation
, 
significant delay
 or 
significant schedule change


Tickets already used for travel


Tickets purchased as part of a group reservation via a travel agency (please contact them directly)




Â 
































Request a Refund or eCredit for a Serious Communicable Disease




























If your doctor advises against travel due to a Serious Communicable Disease, you may qualify for:




A transferable travel credit valid for 5 years, or


In Deltaâs discretion, a refund to your original form of payment




A $100 processing fee will be deducted

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_11.text:
We require health documentation from your attending medical professional to process your request. If you choose not to submit a Serious Communicable Disease eCredit or refund request, but instead do not wish to travel, you can still voluntarily 
cancel your ticket
 and receive an 
eCredit
 valid for 1 year from the date of issuance minus any applicable cancellation fees. Review Eligibility


A Serious Communicable Disease is defined as a disease that can: 




Cause serious health consequences (e

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_12.text:
, breathing problems, organ damage, neurological difficulties, or death)


Be easily transmitted by casual contact (e. , by sitting next to or talking to someone, shaking hands, or touching communal surfaces)




You may be eligible to cancel if:




You have or might have contracted a Serious Communicable Disease, and a medical professional recommends you should not travel around others via public transit

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_13.text:
or


There is a public health emergency at the origin or destination of your itinerary, and you should not travel to protect your health from a Serious Communicable Disease




How to Request an eCredit or a Refund for a Serious Communicable Disease




Download the 
Medical Professional Attestation of Serious Communicable Disease PDF
, opens in a new window


Review eligibility details listed on the form


Have your attending medical professional complete and sign the form


Submit your completed attestation form to Delta for review






































SUBMIT COMPLETED FORM


































Request Reimbursement




























If your flight was 
canceled
 or 
significantly delayed
 by 3 hours or more because of an issue caused by Delta (within our control), please submit a reimbursement request for reasonable meal expenses or for any reasonable hotel and transportation to/from the hotel if you were inconvenienced overnight while away from your home or destination

delta_cancellation_policy_chunk_14.text:
Please note, we are unable to reimburse any expenses for the following:




Expenses incurred due to air traffic control delays


Expenses incurred due to weather delays 


Prepaid expenses 


Hotel expenses for stays in your home city or your destination city 


Alternative transportation to your final destination 


Lost wages 


Ticket costs for missed connections on other airlines 


Child or pet care


Delta Sky ClubÂ® or other lounge fees




If you need to request reimbursement for a delayed, lost or damaged bag, visit our 
Delayed, Lost or Damaged Bags page

Page Name: 
Change Your Flight

                  

               






















































Change or Cancel






Change

delta_change_flight_chunk_1.text:
Weâve simplified travel byâ¯
eliminating change fees
â¯for tickets originating from the United States, Canada and the Caribbean to anywhere in the world (excluding Basic experiences). Itâs easy to cancel, make changes or rebook your flight online prior to departure inâ¯
My Trips. Tickets not changed or canceled prior to departure will have no remaining value. If you change your ticket, sometimes your new flight costs more than your original flight. If this happens, you will need to pay the difference in price

delta_change_flight_chunk_2.text:
Changing a Classic Non-Refundable Ticket












With a non-refundable ticket such as Deltaâs Basic or Classic experience, you can change your flight when you pay the price difference plus any applicable change fee ($0-$400, depending on your travel route and Travel Experience). The Travel Experience (Extra, Classic, or Basic) you selected at the time of purchase will remain the same when changing your ticket

delta_change_flight_chunk_3.text:
Expand All


Collapse All












How to Change a Non-Refundable Ticket






























Ensure you cancel your ticket 
prior to departure


Find Your Trip
 or 
Log In
 and go to 
My Trips


Select the flight you need to change


Click on 'Change or Add Flightsâ


Choose new flight(s)


Complete check out and pay any applicable fees and the price difference between your old flight and your new one


Get your confirmation




If you need additional assistance with making a change, please 
contact us
 and a Reservation Specialist will be able to assist you

delta_change_flight_chunk_4.text:
The United States, Canada & the Caribbean Ticket Change Fees




























                  There are no change fees for travel originating within the 50 United States, Canada, Puerto Rico and the U. Virgin Islands (excluding Delta Basic experiences). Please remember that you will still need to pay the difference in price if your new flight costs more than your original flight. Outside the United States & Canada Ticket Change Fees




























                  For travel outside the 50 United States, Canada, Puerto Rico and the U

delta_change_flight_chunk_5.text:
Virgin Islands, the change fee is $0-$400, depending on theâ¯
origin location and type of fare. Changing an Extra or Refundable Ticket












                  You may change your Extra or 
refundable ticket
 without paying a change fee, provided all fare and ticket rules are met and seats are available. If the ticket price has increased since your original purchase, you will need to pay the difference in price between your original fare to your new fare

delta_change_flight_chunk_6.text:
How to Change an Extra or Refundable Ticket 






























Find Your Trip
 or 
Log In
 and go to 
My Trips


Select the flight you need to change


Click on âChange or Add Flightsâ


Choose new flight(s)


Complete check out and pay any fare difference


Get your confirmation




If you need additional assistance with making a change, please 
contact us
 and a Reservation Specialist will be able to assist you. Changing an Award Ticket












                  It is easy to make changes to your Award Ticket

delta_change_flight_chunk_7.text:
We have simplified travel by eliminating change fees for Award travel within the U. (including Puerto Rico, and the U. S Virgin Islands) and for international travel originating in the United States and Canada. In addition to eliminating change fees, you can now make changes any time 
prior to departure

delta_change_flight_chunk_8.text:
How to Change an Award Ticket






























Find Your Trip
â¯orâ¯
Log In
â¯and go toâ¯
My Trips


Select the flight you need to change


Click on âChange or Add Flightsâ


Choose new flight(s)


Complete check out and pay any fare difference


Get your confirmation




As a reminder, Delta Basic Award Tickets are non-refundable. However, you may change or cancel for a fee assessed in miles, which is deducted from the value of your ticket if you cancel, the remaining mileage balance will be redeposited into your SkyMiles account

delta_change_flight_chunk_9.text:
If you change your Award ticket, the fare difference in miles and cash will be collected at the time of change. If you need additional assistance with making a change, pleaseâ¯
contact us
â¯and a Reservation Specialist will be able to assist you. Third-Party Ticket Changes












                  If you need to change a ticket that Delta didnât issue, such as a ticket you purchased through a hotel or vacation package, additional fees may apply

delta_change_flight_chunk_10.text:
Expand All


Collapse All












Third-Party Ticket Handling Charges 




























Additional third-party charges may apply to the following:




A traditional travel agency


An online travel agency


Another airline, including SkyTeam and codeshare partners


































External Ticket Handling Charges That Will Not Apply 




























For the following, external ticket handling charges will not apply:




Tickets originally issued by Delta â booked through reservation sales, a ticket office, on delta

delta_change_flight_chunk_11.text:
com or on the Fly Delta app


Involuntary changes like cancellations, delays or schedule changes


Tickets issued with military or government fares


Customers completing a SkyMiles upgrade


Same-Day Confirmed 
Ticket Changes


































External Ticket Handling Charge




























The $50 charge is:




Waived if the change is completed on delta

delta_change_flight_chunk_12.text:
com in My Trips


Applied for tickets changed through Reservations Sales


Applied for tickets changed at one of our 
Ticket Office Locations












































































Terms & Ticket Validity












During the ticket change process, we will apply your ticketâs remaining value to your new trip. For travel between and/or within Canada, United States, Puerto Rico and U. Virgin Islands, your ticket must be reissued and all travel completed within one year from the original date of purchase

delta_change_flight_chunk_13.text: For all other travel, your ticket is valid for one year. If travel was started, your ticket must be reissued and all travel must be completed within one year of the start date. If travel was not started, your ticket must be reissued and travel must begin within one year of the original date of purchase

delta_change_flight_chunk_14.text:
Next

, Go to footer note












RULE 24: GOVERNING LAW. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY
, Go to footer note


























RULE 1: 
GENERAL PROVISIONS



 A. Contract of Carriage


WhenÂ When you buy a ticket from or travel on Delta, you enter into a contract with us, and you agree to be bound by its terms. Â  The terms of your contract (the âContract of Carriageâ) are set forth in:




your Ticket

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_3.text:
and


our published fare rules and regulations, which may govern the calculation of the fare and other charges that apply to your itinerary (âFare Rulesâ). Â 




This document is Delta's Domestic Conditions of Carriage. It applies only to travel entirely within the United States of America and states the terms upon which Delta offers to transport passengers. Â 


Any reference to âDeltaâ in this contract refers to Delta Air Lines, the Delta Shuttle, and the Delta Connection carriers. Some flights marketed by Delta may be operated by other carriers

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_4.text:
If any Carrier other than Delta is operating a flight, we will identify that Carrier in our schedules and in written or oral communications with you during the booking process. The terms of transportation applicable to Delta specified in these Conditions of Carriage apply to flights operated by the Delta Connection and Delta Shuttle carriers, and to codeshare flights marketed by Delta. Â 


Delta may act as an agent to issue tickets, check baggage and book reservations for transportation via other Carriers which have interline agreements with Delta

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_5.text:
For interline flights operated by other Carriers, the conditions of carriage of the operating Carrier will apply. Â  Other Carriers may have different terms and conditions applicable to their flights, and these may be obtained directly from the other Carriers. Amendments to Conditions of Carriage




Delta may amend these Conditions of Carriage at any time, except as provided by law. Your travel is governed by the rules that were in effect on the date you purchased your ticket

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_6.text: provided, however, that Delta reserves the right to apply rules currently in effect on the date of your travel where reasonably necessary for operational reasons and where the change in rule does not have a material negative impact upon you. No Delta employee or ticketing agent has the authority to modify any provision of the Conditions of Carriage unless authorized in writing by a Delta corporate officer

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_7.text:
RULE 2: 
SCHEDULES AND OPERATIONS


Delta will exercise reasonable efforts to transport you and your baggage from your origin to your destination with reasonable dispatch, but published schedules, flight times, aircraft types, seat assignments, and similar details reflected in the ticket or Deltaâs published schedules are not guaranteed and form no part of this contract

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_8.text:
Delta may substitute alternate Carriers or aircraft, change its schedules, delay or cancel flights, change seat assignments, and alter or omit stopping places shown on the ticket as required by its operations in Deltaâs sole discretion. Deltaâs sole liability in the event of such changes is set forth in 
Rule 22
, Go to footer note

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_9.text:
Delta is not responsible or liable for making connections, failing to operate any flight according to schedule, changing the schedule or any flight, changing seat assignments or aircraft types, or revising the routings by which Delta carries the passenger from the ticketed origin to destination. RULE 3: 
DEFINITIONS


Animals
, in addition to the usual connotation, include reptiles, birds, and fish

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_10.text:
Applicable Full Fare
 means the one-way fares, whether specifically published or derived by construction, for the class of service designated in the Carrier's official general schedule for the aircraft, or cabin of the aircraft used by the passenger. Carrier
 means any air carrier shown as a participant in this tariff. Â  Â  Â  Â  Â Â 


Co-Terminal
 - Two or more relatively adjacent airports which for the purpose of these fares will be considered the same point. Days
 - Full calendar days, including weekend days and legal holidays (but not including the date that any notice is sent)

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_11.text:
Dependent
 â The spouse and children of U. embassy personnel stationed overseas who are dependent upon such personnel for financial support. Dot Hazardous Materials Regulations
 means the hazardous materials regulations issued by the Materials Transportation Bureau of the Department of Transportation in Title 49 of the Code of Federal Regulations, Parts 171 through 177 (49 CFR 171-177). Fare Component
 - The fare paid for the portion of the itinerary between the origin andÂ destination/Stopover point

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_12.text:
Government Transport Request (GTR)
 - Form used for ticket payment and travel authorization for passengers traveling on official business for the federal government by the U. Group
 means the minimum number of passengers specified in conjunction with the fare as provided for in the applicable fare rules. Â  Less than the minimum number of passengers may not travel at group fares, even upon payment of the minimum number of fares, unless specifically permitted in a given fare rule

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_13.text:
Immediate Family
 means spouse, domestic partner, children, step-children, grandchildren, parents, step-parents, grandparents, brothers, step-brothers, sisters, step-sisters, daughters-in-law, sons-in-law, fathers-in-law, mothers-in-law, aunts, uncles, nieces, nephews, brother-in-law and sisters-in-law. Interlining
 means utilizing the services of more than one Carrier in connection with a particular fare. Military Agencies 
means departments of the Army, Navy and Air Force. the respective academies of the Army, Navy, Air Force and Coast Guard

delta_domestic_contract_chunk_14.text:
Â  The Reserve Officer Training Corps is not included. Military Passenger
 means military personnel of the U. Military Agencies who are on active duty status or who have been discharged from active military service within seven Days of the date of travel. Person with a Disability
 means any person who has a physical or mental impairment that, on a permanent or temporary basis, substantially limits one or more major life activities, has a record of such an impairment, or is regarded as having such an impairment. Â  This term shall be further defined as required by applicable law, including 14 C

delta_flight_help_chunk_1.text:
Looks Like This Page Had a Gate Change












We're sorry, it seems that this page has changed to something different than you were expecting due to a possible URL change or a promotion ending. Not to worry though, we'll do our best to get you where you need to be. To help you take off, you can choose from our most helpful pages below, use our 
search tool

RULE 1
:Â 
General Provisions


A. Â  Contract of Carriage


When you buy a ticket for travel on Delta, you enter into a contract with us, and you agree to be bound by its terms. The terms of your contract (the âContract of Carriageâ) are set forth in:




Your ticket. and


our published fare rules and regulations, which may govern the calculation of the fare and other charges that apply to your itinerary (âFare Rulesâ)

delta_international_contract_chunk_4.text:
Delta Air Lines International Conditions of Carriage are listed below. For past date conditions of carriage, pleaseÂ 
email us
Â specifying which tariff you are requesting and the date of your ticket purchase. This document is Delta's International Conditions of Carriage. It applies to travel on any itinerary for International Carriage and states the terms upon which Delta offers to transport passengers. Travel entirely within the United States of America is governed by Delta's Domestic General Rules Tariff

delta_international_contract_chunk_5.text: Travel between the United States and Canada is governed by our Canadian General Rules Tariff. Any reference to âDeltaâ in this contract refers to Delta Air Lines, the Delta Shuttle, and the Delta Connection carriers. Some flights marketed by Delta may be operated by other Carriers. If any Carrier other than Delta Air Lines is operating a flight, we will identify that Carrier in our schedules and in written or oral communications with you during the booking process

delta_international_contract_chunk_6.text: The terms of transportation applicable to Delta specified in these Conditions of Carriage also apply to flights operated by the Delta Connection and Delta Shuttle carriers, and to codeshare flights where Delta is the Marketing Carrier. Delta may act as an agent to issue tickets, check baggage and book reservations for transportation via other Carriers which have interline agreements with Delta (where Delta is not the Marketing Carrier). For interline flights operated by other Operating Carriers, the conditions of carriage of the Operating Carrier will apply

delta_international_contract_chunk_7.text:
Other Carriers may have different terms and conditions applicable to their flights, and these may be obtained directly from the other Carriers. Some provisions of these Conditions of Carriage apply only to residents or passengers traveling to select countries or regions. All country- and region-specific rules and requirements are set forth in 
Rule 27
 of these Conditions of Carriage. Â  Amendments to Conditions of Carriage




Delta may amend these Conditions of Carriage at any time, except as provided by law

delta_international_contract_chunk_8.text: Your travel is governed by the rules that were in effect on the date you purchased your ticket. provided, however, that Delta reserves the right to apply rules currently in effect on the date of your travel where reasonably necessary for operational reasons and where the change in rule does not have a material negative impact upon you. No Delta employee or ticketing agent has the authority to modify any provision of the Conditions of Carriage unless authorized in writing by a Delta corporate officer

delta_international_contract_chunk_9.text:
RULE 2:Â Â 
SCHEDULES AND OPERATIONS




Delta will exercise reasonable efforts
Â to transport you and your baggage from your origin to your destination with reasonable dispatch, but published schedules, flight times, aircraft types, seat assignments, and similar details reflected in the ticket or Deltaâs published schedules are not guaranteed and form no part of the Contract of Carriage

delta_international_contract_chunk_10.text: Delta may substitute alternate Carriers or aircraft, change its schedules, delay or cancel flights, change seat assignments, and alter or omit stopping places shown on the ticket as required by its operations in Deltaâs sole discretion. Deltaâs sole liability in the event of such changes is set forth in Rule 23

delta_international_contract_chunk_11.text:
Delta is not responsible or liable for making connections, failing to operate any flight according to schedule, changing the schedule for any flight, changing seat assignments or aircraft types, or revising the routings by which Delta carries the passenger from the ticketed origin to destination. RULE 3: 
DEFINITIONS




Animals
, means in addition to the usual connotation, include reptiles, amphibians, birds, and fish

delta_international_contract_chunk_12.text:
Applicable Full Fare
Â means the one-way fare, whether specifically published or derived by construction, for the class of service designated in the Carrier's official general schedule for the aircraft, or cabin of the aircraft used by the passenger. Applicable Law
 means all laws, rules, regulations, orders, treaties, conventions, decrees, and instructions of Carrier, in each case to the extent applicable to the services provided pursuant to the Contract of Carriage. Carrier
 means any air carrier shown as a participant in this tariff

delta_international_contract_chunk_13.text:
Co-Terminal
 means two or more relatively adjacent airports which for the purpose of these fares will be considered the same point. Conjunction Ticket
 means two or more tickets concurrently issued to a passenger, which together constitute a single Contract of Carriage

delta_international_contract_chunk_14.text:
Convention
 means the Convention for the Unification of Certain Rules relating to International Carriage by Air, Signed at Warsaw, October 12, 1929 (Warsaw Convention), Convention for the Unification of Certain Rules for International Carriage by Air, opened for signature on 28 May 1999 (Montreal Convention), or that Convention as amended by the Hague Protocol, 1955 (Hague Convention), whichever may be applicable to carriage hereunder. Days
Â means full calendar days, including weekend days and legal holidays (but not including the date that any notice is sent)
